content:
You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request></user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-03-25</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING START
=======================

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Write</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-03-25T15:32:56.693Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/home/nugad</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/home/nugad/.claude/local-models.sh\",\"content\":\"#!/usr/bin/env bash\\n# Claude Code - Local Model Switcher\\n# Usage: source ~/.claude/local-models.sh [model|cloud]\\n# Models available on this machine via Ollama\\n\\nOLLAMA_URL=\\\"http://localhost:11434\\\"\\n\\n# Available local models\\ndeclare -A MODELS=(\\n  [llama3.2]=\\\"llama3.2:3b\\\"          # 2.0 GB - fast, general purpose\\n  [phi3.5]=\\\"phi3.5:3.8b\\\"            # 2.2 GB - fast, efficient\\n  [mistral]=\\\"mistral:7b\\\"            # 4.4 GB - strong general reasoning\\n  [deepseek-coder]=\\\"deepseek-coder:6.7b\\\"  # 3.8 GB - coding focused\\n  [deepseek-r1]=\\\"deepseek-r1:7b\\\"    # 4.7 GB - reasoning/thinking model\\n)\\n\\nuse_local() {\\n  local model_key=\\\"${1:-deepseek-coder}\\\"\\n  local model_name=\\\"${MODELS[$model_key]}\\\"\\n\\n  if [[ -z \\\"$model_name\\\" ]]; then\\n    echo \\\"Unknown model: $model_key\\\"\\n    echo \\\"Available: ${!MODELS[*]}\\\"\\n    return 1\\n  fi\\n\\n  # Check Ollama is running\\n  if ! curl -sf \\\"$OLLAMA_URL/api/tags\\\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\\n    echo \\\"Ollama not running. Start it with: ollama serve\\\"\\n    return 1\\n  fi\\n\\n  export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama\\n  export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=\\\"$OLLAMA_URL\\\"\\n  export ANTHROPIC_MODEL=\\\"$model_name\\\"\\n\\n  echo \\\"Switched to LOCAL: $model_name\\\"\\n  echo \\\"Run 'claude' to start a session with this model.\\\"\\n}\\n\\nuse_cloud() {\\n  unset ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN\\n  unset ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL\\n  unset ANTHROPIC_MODEL\\n  echo \\\"Switched to CLOUD (Anthropic API)\\\"\\n}\\n\\nlist_models() {\\n  echo \\\"=== Local Models (Ollama) ===\\\"\\n  for key in \\\"${!MODELS[@]}\\\"; do\\n    printf \\\"  %-18s %s\\\\n\\\" \\\"$key\\\" \\\"${MODELS[$key]}\\\"\\n  done | sort\\n  echo \\\"\\\"\\n  echo \\\"Usage:\\\"\\n  echo \\\"  source ~/.claude/local-models.sh llama3.2\\\"\\n  echo \\\"  source ~/.claude/local-models.sh deepseek-coder\\\"\\n  echo \\\"  source ~/.claude/local-models.sh cloud\\\"\\n}\\n\\n# If called with an argument, act on it\\nif [[ \\\"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\\\" != \\\"${0}\\\" ]]; then\\n  # Script is being sourced\\n  case \\\"${1:-}\\\" in\\n    cloud)       use_cloud ;;\\n    list|\\\"\\\")     list_models ;;\\n    *)           use_local \\\"$1\\\" ;;\\n  esac\\nelse\\n  echo \\\"This script must be sourced, not executed.\\\"\\n  echo \\\"Usage: source ~/.claude/local-models.sh [model|cloud|list]\\\"\\nfi\\n\"}"</parameters>

Start it with: ollama serve\\\"\\n    return 1\\n  fi\\n\\n  export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama\\n  export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=\\\"$OLLAMA_URL\\\"\\n  export ANTHROPIC_MODEL=\\\"$model_name\\\"\\n\\n  echo \\\"Switched to LOCAL: $model_name\\\"\\n  echo \\\"Run 'claude' to start a session with this model.\\\"\\n}\\n\\nuse_cloud() {\\n  unset ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN\\n  unset ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL\\n  unset ANTHROPIC_MODEL\\n  echo \\\"Switched to CLOUD (Anthropic API)\\\"\\n}\\n\\nlist_models() {\\n  echo \\\"=== Local Models (Ollama) ===\\\"\\n  for key in \\\"${!MODELS[@]}\\\"; do\\n    printf \\\"  %-18s %s\\\\n\\\" \\\"$key\\\" \\\"${MODELS[$key]}\\\"\\n  done | sort\\n  echo \\\"\\\"\\n  echo \\\"Usage:\\\"\\n  echo \\\"  source ~/.claude/local-models.sh llama3.2\\\"\\n  echo \\\"  source ~/.claude/local-models.sh deepseek-coder\\\"\\n  echo \\\"  source ~/.claude/local-models.sh cloud\\\"\\n}\\n\\n# If called with an argument, act on it\\nif [[ \\\"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\\\" != \\\"${0}\\\" ]]; then\\n  # Script is being sourced\\n  case \\\"${1:-}\\\" in\\n    cloud)       use_cloud ;;\\n    list|\\\"\\\")     list_models ;;\\n    *)           use_local \\\"$1\\\" ;;\\n  esac\\nelse\\n  echo \\\"This script must be sourced, not executed.\\\"\\n  echo \\\"Usage: source ~/.claude/local-models.sh [model|cloud|list]\\\"\\nfi\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null}"</outcome>

content:
--- MODE SWITCH: PROGRESS SUMMARY ---
Do NOT output <observation> tags. This is a summary request, not an observation request.
Your response MUST use <summary> tags ONLY. Any <observation> output will be discarded.

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
All set. Here's a summary of what's configured:

## Your Local Models (Ollama v0.18.2)

| Alias | Model | Size | Best for |
|---|---|---|---|
| `llama3.2` | llama3.2:3b | 2.0 GB | Fast, general use |
| `phi3.5` | phi3.5:3.8b | 2.2 GB | Fast, efficient |
| `mistral` | mistral:7b | 4.4 GB | General reasoning |
| `deepseek-coder` | deepseek-coder:6.7b | 3.8 GB | Coding tasks |
| `deepseek-r1` | deepseek-r1:7b | 4.7 GB | Reasoning/thinking |

## Usage (after reloading shell)

```bash
# Switch to a local model
claude-local deepseek-coder   # coding tasks
claude-local deepseek-r1      # reasoning tasks  
claude-local mistral           # general use

# Then launch Claude Code as normal
claude

# Switch back to cloud
claude-cloud
```

Or directly without reloading:
```bash
source ~/.claude/local-models.sh deepseek-coder
```

content.thinking:
Now I have an observation to record. Let me analyze what happened:

**What happened:** A bash script was created at `/home/nugad/.claude/local-models.sh`

**What it does:** This is a local model switcher for Claude Code that allows users to:
1. Switch between local Ollama models and cloud Anthropic API
2. Manage environment variables (ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_MODEL)
3. Lists available local models with their sizes and descriptions
4. Validates Ollama is running before switching
5. Provides a convenient interface for switching between 5 local models:
   - llama3.2:3b (2.0 GB)
   - phi3.5:3.8b (2.2 GB)
   - mistral:7b (4.4 GB)
   - deepseek-coder:6.7b (3.8 GB)
   - deepseek-r1:7b (4.7 GB)

**Type:** This is a "feature" - new capability added to Claude Code to use local models

**Facts:**
- Script created at ~/.claude/local-models.sh for switching between local and cloud models
- Supports 5 Ollama models: llama3.2, phi3.5, mistral, deepseek-coder, deepseek-r1
- use_local() function sets ANTHROPIC_* environment variables to point to Ollama (localhost:11434)
- use_cloud() function unsets environment variables to restore Anthropic API usage
- Script validates Ollama service is running before switching to local models

**Narrative:** 
A bash utility script was implemented to enable Claude Code to work with local Ollama models as an alternative to the cloud Anthropic API. The script provides three main functions: use_local() to switch to a local model by setting appropriate environment variables, use_cloud() to revert to cloud API, and list_models() to display available options. 

The script must be sourced rather than executed to properly modify the shell environment, and it includes validation checks to ensure Ollama is running before attempting to use local models. This approach enables seamless switching between local and cloud-based model backends through environment variable manipulation.

**Concepts:**
- how-it-works: environment variable manipulation for model backend switching
- pattern: reusable model switching approach
- what-changed: new capability added

**Files:**
- Modified: /home/nugad/.claude/local-models.sh (created)
- Read: none

```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Local Model Switcher for Claude Code with Ollama Integration</title>
  <subtitle>Bash utility enables switching between local Ollama models and cloud Anthropic API for Claude Code sessions.</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Script created at ~/.claude/local-models.sh provides model switching capability between local Ollama and cloud API</fact>
    <fact>Five local models configured: llama3.2 (2GB), phi3.5 (2.2GB), mistral (7b/4.4GB), deepseek-coder (6.7b/3.8GB), deepseek-r1 (7b/4.7GB)</fact>
    <fact>use_local() function exports ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434, and ANTHROPIC_MODEL to selected model</fact>
    <fact>use_cloud() function unsets ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, and ANTHROPIC_MODEL to restore cloud API behavior</fact>
    <fact>Script validates Ollama service availability via curl health check before switching to local models</fact>
    <fact>Must be sourced (not executed) to modify parent shell environment variables</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>A bash utility script was implemented to enable Claude Code to work with local Ollama models as an alternative to the cloud Anthropic API. The script provides three main functions: use_local() switches to a specified local model by setting ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, and ANTHROPIC_MODEL environment variables; use_cloud() reverts to cloud API by unsetting these variables; and list_models() displays all available models with metadata. Five models are pre-configured with size and purpose information (ranging from 2GB to 4.7GB). The script includes error handling to validate Ollama service availability and unknown model keys. It uses bash associative arrays for model configuration and must be sourced rather than executed to properly modify the shell environment. This enables offline/local development workflows and cost optimization.</narrative>

    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

- claude-cloud alias executes 'source ~/.claude/local-models.sh cloud' (switches to cloud API)
- Aliases appended to existing shell configuration files for persistent availability

**Narrative:**
Shell aliases were configured in both bash and zsh configuration files to provide convenient access to the local model switcher. The claude-local alias sources the local-models.sh script (which displays available models when called without arguments), while claude-cloud immediately switches back to the cloud Anthropic API. Both aliases are added to ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc to ensure availability across shell types and persist in future sessions.

  <completed>Successfully verified claude-local and claude-cloud aliases are active and functional in interactive shell mode. Configuration is complete with both aliases pointing to correct ~/.claude/local-models.sh script invocations. Also discovered pre-existing claude-status alias in the configuration.</completed>
  <next_steps>Configuration and verification complete. System is ready for use. User can switch to local Ollama models with claude-local followed by model name, or return to cloud API with claude-cloud command.</next_steps>
